summary: Cathy O'Neil recommends a lecture by David Kung examining gender and racial achievement gaps in mathematics. She notes the lecture provides historical perspective on math's 'sad history' regarding these disparities and advocates for ending passive lecturing as a teaching method. The post is brief, serving primarily as a pointer to Kung's more detailed analysis.
key_points: David Kung has researched gender and racial achievement gaps in mathematics education
Mathematics has a troubled historical record regarding inclusion and equity
The lecture argues for abandoning passive lecturing in favor of more engaging teaching methods
key_insight: Mathematics education has historically failed women and minorities, and reforming teaching methods away from passive lecturing is part of addressing these gaps.
